metadata.notes: DEMO file. Replace each result block with values from your own analyses. Rows whose `derive_from` is set in SP_DEFAULT_ROWS will be auto-populated from atlas state (cs_breakpoints, candidate list) and don't need to appear here unless you want to override the derivation.

summary_rows.null_comparison: size-matched random intervals on same chromosomes
summary_rows.interpretation: C. gariepinus inversions trend gene-poor; orthologous C. macrocephalus regions do not — consistent with inversions capturing distinct chromosomal domains in the African catfish lineage.

summary_rows.statistic: GO / KEGG enrichment among inversion-internal genes

african_result.label: 12 KEGG terms enriched (q<0.05): immune response, hormone signalling, lipid metabolism

bighead_result.label: orthologous gene set; no Mac-side cohort enrichment available

summary_rows.interpretation: Inversion cargo enriched for immune, hormone, and metabolism functions — candidate breeding-relevant biology. Causal claims require functional validation.

african_result.label: 18 candidates (immune/growth/stress/reproduction) inside inversions vs 7.8 expected (Fisher p=0.0034)

summary_rows.interpretation: Possible breeding-relevant cargo enriched in structural haplotypes. Candidate, not causal.
Inversion regions preserve divergent haplotype backgrounds in the broodstock, consistent with structural-haplotype heterozygosity.

summary_rows.statistic: ROH / FROH overlap with inversion intervals

african_result.label: ROH overlap 6% inside inversions vs 12% genome-wide (p=0.045)

summary_rows.interpretation: Inversion haplotypes are NOT preferentially in ROH — consistent with their persistence as polymorphic structural haplotypes rather than collapse to homozygosity in inbreeding lines.

african_result.label: carriers carry 1.42× deleterious-variant burden (SIFT4G + VESM_650M, p=0.034)

summary_rows.interpretation: Some structural haplotypes appear to carry slightly elevated deleterious-variant burden — a candidate breeding-risk signal worth tracking when selecting carrier vs non-carrier lines.

summary_rows.statistic: FST / dXY between inversion structural states

african_result.label: FST = 0.308 between inversion states vs 0.041 in flanks (LG28 sub-telomeric, K=3 hatchery groups)

summary_rows.interpretation: Inversion structural states are strongly differentiated, supporting them as biological haplotypes rather than analytical noise.
